Note: This is JSON is enhanced by AI to provide structured data for the Canton MCP server. Kindly maintain the same structure and formatting when updating.

DEPRECATED.name: Daml Assistant (daml-assistant)
DEPRECATED.note: daml-assistant is ONLY for legacy Splice 0.4.x / Canton 3.3. For Canton 3.4+ / Splice 0.5.0+, always use DPM.
DEPRECATED.installReplacement:
Mac/Linux: curl https://get.digitalasset.com/install/install.sh | sh
Windows: Download the Windows installer from the DPM docs.

DEPRECATED.name: Daml Hub
DEPRECATED.note: Daml Hub has been discontinued. Use Canton Sandbox for local dev, CN Quickstart LocalNet, or DevNet/TestNet.

DEPRECATED.name: daml start
DEPRECATED.replacement: dpm sandbox + dpm build + Ledger API
DEPRECATED.note: 'daml start' is legacy. Use 'dpm sandbox' for local ledger, 'dpm build' to compile, JSON/gRPC API for upload/allocate.

DEPRECATED.name: daml build
DEPRECATED.note: Use 'dpm build' to compile. 'dpm build --all' for multi-package projects.

DEPRECATED.name: daml test
DEPRECATED.note: Use 'dpm test' for Daml Script tests. 'dpm test --files <file>' for specific files.

DEPRECATED.name: daml new
DEPRECATED.note: Use 'dpm new <name>' or 'dpm new <name> --template <t>' for templates like daml-intro-compose.

DEPRECATED.name: Navigator
DEPRECATED.note: Navigator is discontinued. Build frontends using the JSON Ledger API (port 7575) or gRPC API.

DEPRECATED.name: Daml Triggers
DEPRECATED.note: Daml Triggers deprecated. Implement automation in your backend using gRPC/JSON Ledger API update stream.

DEPRECATED.name: daml ledger upload-dar
DEPRECATED.replacement: JSON / gRPC Ledger API or Declarative API
DEPRECATED.note: Use JSON Ledger API or gRPC PackageService to upload DARs.

DEPRECATED.name: daml ledger allocate-parties
DEPRECATED.note: Use the JSON Ledger API (port 7575) or gRPC PartyManagementService.

DEPRECATED.name: splice-wallet-payments subscription
DEPRECATED.note: CC subscription workflow in splice-wallet-payments is deprecated. Use token standard DVP workflows.

DEPRECATED.name: prune_to_offset (PQS SQL function)
DEPRECATED.note: Deprecated since 3.5.0. Known to cause deadlocks and performance bottlenecks.

dpm.name: Digital Asset Package Manager (DPM)
dpm.description: Primary CLI for all Canton/Daml development. Drop-in replacement for deprecated daml-assistant. Runs on Windows, macOS, Linux. Requires JDK 17+ and VS Code.
dpm.install:
Mac/Linux: curl https://get.digitalasset.com/install/install.sh | sh
Windows: Download Windows installer from DPM docs.
Install dir: ${HOME}/.dpm/ (Mac/Linux) or %APPDATA%/.dpm/ (Windows).
Set DPM_HOME to customize.

commands.dpm new <n>: Create new Daml project from template
commands.dpm new <n> --template <t>: Create from specific template (daml-intro-compose, daml-intro-test, json-tests, etc.)
commands.dpm build: Compile Daml project into DAR file (.daml/dist/<n>-<ver>.dar)
commands.dpm build --all: Build all packages in multi-package project (topological order)
commands.dpm sandbox: Start Canton sandbox (JSON API on 7575, gRPC on 6866)
commands.dpm damlc inspect-dar <f>: Inspect DAR contents and package hashes

dpm.config: daml.yaml (per-package), multi-package.yaml (multi-package builds), ${DPM_HOME}/dpm-config.yaml (global). Supports ${ENV_VAR} interpolation. Env overrides: DPM_SDK_VERSION, DPM_REGISTRY, DPM_LOG_LEVEL, DAML_PACKAGE.

canton_sandbox.name: Canton Sandbox
canton_sandbox.description: Local Canton ledger. Start via 'dpm sandbox'. JSON API port 7575, gRPC port 6866.

create_canton_app.name: create-canton-app
create_canton_app.description: Canton Foundation CLI scaffolding: npx create-canton-app@latest my-app. Auto-detects SDK. Token, Escrow, Empty templates.

canton_playbox.name: Canton Playbox
canton_playbox.description: Browser-based Daml IDE. 20+ templates, auto-save, no install. Best for first exploration.

vscode_daml.name: Daml VS Code Extension
vscode_daml.description: Syntax highlighting, inline script results, error checking, code coverage. Launch: dpm studio.
vscode_daml.install: Search 'Daml' in VS Code Extensions or run 'dpm studio'

pqs.name: Participant Query Store (PQS)
pqs.description: Ingests ledger data into PostgreSQL for SQL querying. Executable: 'scribe'. Enables SQL on Daml ledger state (contracts) and history (events). Point-in-time queries, party/template filtering, JSONB columns.
pqs.install:
dpm install <version> (includes scribe.jar)
Locate: dpm resolve | grep scribe
Docker: europe-docker.pkg.dev/da-images/public/docker/participant-query-store:<version>
pqs.requirements: PQS: 4GB RAM, 4 CPU. PostgreSQL: 8GB RAM, 8 CPU. JDK 17+. Tune PostgreSQL (default Docker config is inadequate).
pqs.security: Only canReadAs/readAsAnyParty needed. No actAs or admin access. Restrict DB users to minimum privileges.

daml_shell.name: Daml Shell
daml_shell.description: Interactive Daml exploration tool. Connects to PQS PostgreSQL database by default. Part of CN Quickstart modules.

main.title: Canton App Dev Docs (v3.4)
main.description: Main entry point for Canton Network app development.

tutorial_json_api.description: Create parties, contracts, query ledger with curl.

tutorial_json_api_ts.title: Get Started: JSON API + TypeScript
tutorial_json_api_ts.description: openapi-fetch client generation, Daml bindings, TypeScript.

tutorial_smart_contracts.title: Smart Contract Development (Daml)
tutorial_smart_contracts.description: Full Daml tutorial: templates, choices, signatories, observers, constraints, testing, composition, privacy.

tutorial_choices.title: Choices
tutorial_choices.description: Choices as methods, controller, consuming vs nonconsuming.

tutorial_constraints.title: Constraints
tutorial_constraints.description: ensure keyword, assert/assertMsg, time on ledger.

tutorial_testing.title: Test Daml Contracts
tutorial_testing.description: Multi-package testing, dpm test, trace/debug, choice coverage.

tutorial_dependencies.title: Dependencies
tutorial_dependencies.description: DAR files, package hashes, dependencies vs data-dependencies.

best_practices.description: SDLC: design, develop, test, deploy, operations for multi-party apps.

daml_stdlib.title: Daml Standard Library
daml_stdlib.description: Always include daml-prim and daml-stdlib in dependencies.

dpm_config.title: DPM Configuration
dpm_config.description: daml.yaml, multi-package.yaml, env var interpolation, SDK overrides.

grpc_ledger_api.description: gRPC Ledger API on Participant node. Port 6866.

splice_overview.title: Splice Overview
splice_overview.description: Canton Coin, CNS, Global Synchronizer governance.

splice_architecture.title: Splice Architecture
splice_architecture.description: Seven Daml packages: splice-amulet (CC), splice-dso-governance, splice-wallet, etc.

splice_http_apis.title: Splice HTTP APIs
splice_http_apis.description: Scan API (SV nodes), Validator API. OpenAPI specs.

splice_daml_apis.title: Splice Daml APIs
splice_daml_apis.description: Token standard (CIP-0056), featured app markers (CIP-0047), WalletUserProxy.

token_standard.title: Token Standard (CIP-0056)
token_standard.description: Standard APIs: holdings, FOP transfers, DVP allocations, metadata.

token_standard_apis.title: Token Standard APIs
token_standard_apis.description: Daml + OpenAPI refs: Metadata, Holding, Transfer, Allocation.

featured_app.title: Featured App Rewards
featured_app.description: CIP-0047: earn app rewards with FeaturedAppActivityMarker.

cc_transfer.title: CC Transfer Types
cc_transfer.description: Legacy offer, two-step token standard, one-step TransferPreapproval. No fees (CIP-0078).

validator_onboarding.title: Validator Onboarding
validator_onboarding.description: Steps to onboard as a Canton Network validator.

external_party.title: External Party Onboarding
external_party.description: External parties with own signing keys. Python examples.

devnet.description: Shared dev network. Self-feature apps for testing.

testnet.title: TestNet
testnet.description: Stable test network. Ethereum Sepolia for bridge testing.

scan_api.title: Scan API Discovery
scan_api.description: Find Scan API URLs for all networks.

github_splice.title: Splice (GitHub)
github_splice.description: Open-source: CC, CNS, governance, token standard.

pqs.title: Participant Query Store (PQS)
pqs.description: SQL read layer for Canton. PostgreSQL. State + audit trail. JSONB. Configure, secure, operate, optimize.

tutorial_functional.title: Functional Programming 101 (Daml)
tutorial_functional.description: Functions, if/else, folds, recursion, when, pattern matching, typeclasses. Daml borrows from Haskell.

tutorial_data_types.title: Daml Data Types
tutorial_data_types.description: Records, variants, Optional, enums, tuples, Eq/Show/Ord deriving, pattern matching.

tutorial_stdlib.title: Daml Standard Library Guide
tutorial_stdlib.description: DA.List, DA.Optional, DA.Tuple, DA.Either, DA.Action, DA.Text, DA.Time, Functor, Foldable, Traversable.

splice_localnet.title: Splice LocalNet (Docker Compose)
splice_localnet.description: 3 participants (app-provider/app-user/sv), PostgreSQL, wallet/sv/scan UIs. Dev/testing only.

splice_configuring.title: Configuring Deployed Splice Apps
splice_configuring.description: HOCON config via ADDITIONAL_CONFIG_* env vars. Custom bootstrap scripts via OVERRIDE_BOOTSTRAP_SCRIPT.

traffic_fees.title: Synchronizer Traffic Fees
traffic_fees.description: Free tier, extraTrafficPrice USD/MB, MemberTraffic contracts, top-up mechanics.

steps_in_round.title: Steps in a Mining Round
steps_in_round.description: Mining round lifecycle: open, issuing, close. ~2.5 min per round.

users_parties_wallets.title: Users, Parties, and Wallets
users_parties_wallets.description: Relationship between ledger API users, Daml parties, and Splice wallet instances.

wallet_integration.title: Wallet/Custody/Exchange Integration
wallet_integration.description: Key differences for integrators: your node = your state, TransferPreapproval for deposits, one party per account.

sv_super_validator_k8s.title: Super Validator Node (Kubernetes)
sv_super_validator_k8s.description: SV deployment: Scan, Sequencer, Mediator, CometBFT, wallet. IP whitelisting required.

what_is_canton.title: What is Canton Network?
what_is_canton.summary: Privacy-enabled blockchain for institutional use. Apps use the Canton ledger for multi-org business processes with automatic state sync and privacy. Apps compose on-ledger (atomic Daml transactions) and off-ledger (APIs). Used by Hashnote, Brale, SocGen for tokenized RWAs.
key_points: Multi-party apps with automatic state synchronization
Privacy by design — data only shared with relevant parties
Not all apps need custom Daml — can build on other apps' code or APIs
Complementary to public blockchains — institutional privacy use cases

privacy_model.title: Canton Privacy Model
privacy_model.summary: Selective visibility — participants only see transactions they're party to. NO shared global state. Synchronizer nodes CANNOT decrypt payloads.
key_points: Principle 1: Parties see actions they have a stake in
Principle 2: Every party seeing an action sees its transitive consequences
Synchronizer only sees encrypted envelopes and routing metadata
Data minimization on need-to-know basis

daml_language.title: Daml Smart Contract Language
daml_language.summary: Functional smart contract language. Templates = classes, contracts = objects, choices = methods. Compiled to Daml-LF bytecode, packaged as DAR files.
key_points: template: contract schema. 'with' block for fields, 'where' block for logic
signatory: parties required to create/archive. Every contract needs at least one
observer: parties who can see but not act. Guaranteed to see create/archive
choice: permissioned function. controller specifies who can exercise
nonconsuming: choice that doesn't archive the contract
ensure: template precondition (Bool). e.g. ensure amount > 0.0
assert/assertMsg: runtime checks in choices
Daml Script: testing. allocateParty, submit, createCmd, exerciseCmd, submitMustFail
daml.yaml: project config. Always include daml-prim, daml-stdlib in dependencies
DAR file: compiled artifact in .daml/dist/. Self-contained like a JAR
Package hash: the ONLY reliable identifier for deployed packages (hex string)

party_id.title: Party IDs
party_id.summary: NOT like ETH addresses. Cannot generate locally — need a participant node. Format: name::fingerprint. Use parties economically.
key_points: Format: <hint>::<namespace_fingerprint> e.g. alice::12200b11a778...
Party Name: max 185 chars [a-zA-Z0-9_], unique in namespace
Fingerprint: from validator's key (internal) or external signing key
Allocate via Admin API or JSON Ledger API POST /v2/parties/allocate
External parties retain own signing keys for security
Avoid ephemeral parties — one party per wallet/account

synchronizer.title: Synchronizer
synchronizer.summary: Provides sequencing (ordered encrypted communication) and mediation (two-phase commit). NOT a validator.
key_points: Sequencer: ordered, encrypted message delivery
Mediator: aggregates confirmations, approves/rejects transactions
Global Synchronizer: shared synchronizer for Canton Network
Cannot decrypt payloads — privacy by design

utxo_model.title: UTXO Model
utxo_model.summary: Each Daml contract = one UTXO. Distributed only to stakeholder nodes.
key_points: 1:1 correspondence: Daml contract = UTXO
Active Contract Set (ACS) = unspent outputs for a party
Contracts immutable — update by archive old + create new
All transactions atomic — commit fully or fail

transaction_model.title: Transaction Model
transaction_model.summary: Transactions are atomic trees of actions. Requester knows all consequences — no surprises.
key_points: Steps: interpret → blind → submit → validate → commit → confirm → complete
All transactions atomic
Required authorizers: create=signatories, exercise=controllers, archive=signatories
Authorization rule: required authorizers ⊆ parent transaction's authorizers

token_standard.title: Token Standard (CIP-0056)
token_standard.summary: Standard APIs for Canton tokens. Inspired by ERC20 but for UTXO model with privacy. CC implements the full standard.
key_points: Token Metadata: name, symbol, totalSupply
Holdings: portfolio view from validator node
Transfer Instruction: FOP peer-to-peer transfers
Allocation: DVP atomic settlement
All amounts use Daml Decimal (10 decimal places)
No unconstrained allowances — allocations cover this
CC limitation: 10-min submission delay (24h targeted)

splice_packages.title: Splice Package Architecture
splice_packages.summary: Seven Daml packages for CC, CNS, and governance.
key_points: splice-util, splice-amulet (CC), splice-amulet-name-service (CNS)
splice-dso-governance, splice-validator-lifecycle
splice-wallet-payments (subscription deprecated), splice-wallet (being replaced by token standard)
Use Daml interfaces for decoupling from Splice upgrades

featured_apps.title: Featured App Rewards
featured_apps.summary: Featured apps earn CC rewards. Create FeaturedAppActivityMarker for important events.
key_points: Apply via tokenomics committee
After CIP-0078: only featured apps get rewards (~$1 USD/marker)
Create markers for: RWA lock/unlock, transfers, mint/burn
NOT for intermediate/propose steps
Self-feature on DevNet for testing
WalletUserProxy helps wallet providers earn rewards

canton_vs_evm.title: Canton vs EVM
canton_vs_evm.summary: Fundamentally different. Complementary to public blockchains — institutional privacy use cases can't exist on transparent networks.

differences.canton: Your node has only your data

wallet_integration.title: Wallet/Custody/Exchange Integration
wallet_integration.summary: Key differences for integrators: your node has your state, parties have cost, TransferPreapproval for deposits.
key_points: Your node has your state — no shared RPC like Ethereum
Need a validator node (self-host or node-as-a-service)
Wallets safekeep DATA and assets — preserving customer privacy
One party per wallet/account, avoid ephemeral parties
TransferPreapproval for receiving deposits (1-step transfers)
Integration order: node → DevNet parties → token standard → TestNet → MainNet

pqs.title: Participant Query Store (PQS)
pqs.summary: Scalable SQL read layer for Canton. Ingests ledger data into PostgreSQL via 'scribe'. Query contracts (state) and events (audit trail) with SQL.
key_points: Query Daml ledger via SQL using Daml identifiers (mypackage:My.Module:MyTemplate)
Point-in-time or latest-available basis
Filter by party and contract template
JSONB columns — create custom indexes for optimization
Crash-tolerant, restartable safely
Security: only canReadAs/readAsAnyParty needed — no actAs/admin
Complements Ledger API: PQS for reads/analytics, Ledger API for commands
CN Quickstart includes preconfigured PQS module

traffic_fees.title: Synchronizer Traffic Fees
traffic_fees.summary: Canton charges traffic-based fees instead of gas. Free tier via burstAmount/burstWindow, then extraTrafficPrice in USD/MB paid in CC.
key_points: Free tier: burstAmount bytes per burstWindow (restored after inactivity)
Extra traffic: USD/MB, paid in CC at current exchange rate
Exchange rate: median of SV votes on OpenMiningRound contracts
readVsWriteScalingFactor: per-recipient delivery cost in basis points
MemberTraffic contracts track purchased traffic on-ledger
minTopupAmount: minimum purchase to amortize SV processing cost
Configured by SVs via DSO on-ledger voting

scan_api_detail.title: Scan API (Detail)
scan_api_detail.summary: Public HTTP API on SV nodes. Network-wide view of CC activity, mining rounds, registry metadata. Essential for wallets and apps building transfers.
key_points: Discovery: https://sync.global/sv-network/
Registry metadata: GET /registry/metadata/v1/info → adminId, supportedApis
Instruments: GET /registry/metadata/v1/instruments → id, name, symbol, totalSupply
Transfer factory: POST /registry/transfer-instruction/v1/transfer-factory → factoryId, transferKind, choiceContext, disclosedContracts
CC price: POST /api/scan/v0 with cached_open_mining_round_trip_with_uses
SV versions: GET /api/scan/v0/scans → list of all SV scan URLs
BFT reads: query 2/3+ of SV scans for Byzantine fault tolerance
GSF example: https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global

localnet.title: LocalNet (Splice Docker Compose)
localnet.summary: Full local Canton Network for dev/testing. 3 validators, PostgreSQL, wallet/sv/scan UIs behind nginx.
key_points: Validators: app-provider (3xxx ports), app-user (2xxx), sv (4xxx)
Wallet UIs: wallet.localhost:2000 (user), :3000 (provider)
Scan UI: scan.localhost:4000
SV UI: sv.localhost:4000
Default users: app-user, app-provider, sv
Swagger UI: port 9090 (--profile swagger-ui)
Rounds take up to 1 hour (6 rounds) to appear in Scan UI
Single PostgreSQL for all components
Modules: keycloak (optional), splice-onboarding, pqs, observability, daml-shell

mining_rounds.title: Mining Rounds
mining_rounds.summary: Canton's time-division mechanism. Rounds advance every ~2.5 minutes. Each round has an associated CC/USD exchange rate set by SV median vote.
key_points: ~2.5 minute intervals
OpenMiningRound contracts hold current exchange rate
SVs vote on conversion rate (median wins)
Rewards issued per round to validators and featured apps
After CIP-0078: no CC transfer fees, only featured app rewards

local.name: LocalNet (CN Quickstart)
local.description: Full local Canton Network via Docker Compose.
local.setup: git clone https://github.com/digital-asset/cn-quickstart && make install && make start

devnet.name: DevNet
devnet.description: Shared dev network. Resets periodically. Self-feature apps for testing.

testnet.name: TestNet
testnet.description: Stable test network. Ethereum Sepolia for xReserve bridge.

usdc_details.note: Ethereum MainNet → Canton MainNet via Circle xReserve

COMMUNITY.warning: Price speculation in Slack → immediate removal.

VERSIONS.note: daml-assistant still works for legacy Canton 3.3 but NOT recommended for new projects.

ZENITH.name: Zenith
ZENITH.description: Shared Reth-based EVM on Canton. EVM payloads wrap into native Canton transactions atomically.
key_points: NOT an L2 rollup — atomic composition with Canton L1 via external_call()
Dual-leg finality
Built by Zenith, not Canton Foundation
EVM devs interact with Canton without learning Daml

FAQ.question: How do I install the Canton SDK?
FAQ.answer:
Install DPM:

Mac/Linux: curl https://get.digitalasset.com/install/install.sh | sh
Windows: Download Windows installer from DPM docs.
Prereqs: JDK 17+, VS Code
Verify: dpm version --active

DO NOT use daml-assistant — deprecated for Canton 3.4+.

FAQ.question: How do I create a new Daml project?
FAQ.answer:
dpm new my-project

With template: dpm new my-project --template daml-intro-compose
Templates: daml-intro-compose, daml-intro-test, daml-intro-constraints, json-tests

Then: cd my-project && dpm build

FAQ.question: How do I start a local Canton ledger?
FAQ.answer:
Quick: dpm sandbox (JSON API on 7575)
Full network: git clone cn-quickstart && make install && make start
Browser: https://playbox.canton.network

FAQ.question: How do I create a party?
How do I create a contract?
FAQ.answer:
1. Write Daml template
2. dpm build
3. dpm sandbox -c config.conf
4. curl localhost:7575/v2/commands/submit-and-wait -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d @create.json

Tutorial: https://docs.digitalasset.com/build/3.4/tutorials/json-api/canton_and_the_json_ledger_api.html

FAQ.question: How do I query active contracts?
FAQ.answer:
curl localhost:7575/v2/state/active-contracts -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d @acs.json | jq

Set activeAtOffset to completionOffset from last submit.
Check offset: curl localhost:7575/v2/state/ledger-end

FAQ.question: What is daml.yaml?
FAQ.answer:
Project config:

sdk-version: 3.4.x
name: my-project
source: daml
version: 1.0.0
dependencies:
  - daml-prim
  - daml-stdlib
  - daml-script

Supports ${ENV_VAR} interpolation. For multi-package: create multi-package.yaml listing sub-packages.

FAQ.question: What is a DAR file?
FAQ.answer:
Compiled Daml artifact (.daml/dist/). Contains Daml-LF bytecode + all dependencies. Self-contained like a JAR.

Inspect: dpm damlc inspect-dar myfile.dar
JSON: dpm damlc inspect-dar --json myfile.dar (main_package_id = package hash)

Package hash is the ONLY reliable identifier for deployed packages.

FAQ.question: How do I test Daml contracts?
FAQ.answer:
VS Code: click 'Script results' lens for table/transaction views
CLI: dpm test (all scripts) or dpm test --files Token_Test.daml

Key functions: allocateParty, submit, createCmd, exerciseCmd, archiveCmd, submitMustFail
Provides choice coverage info.

FAQ.question: Canton vs Ethereum?
FAQ.answer: Selective visibility (no global state), party-as-vault, UTXO model, two-phase commit, Daml (not Solidity). Synchronizer can't read transaction data. Complementary to public blockchains — institutional privacy use cases.

FAQ.question: How do I transfer tokens?
FAQ.answer:
Token Standard (CIP-0056):
1. TransferPreapproval: receiver preapproves (1-step deposits)
2. Transfer Instruction API: FOP transfers
3. Allocation API: DVP settlement

CC transfers have NO fees (CIP-0078).
Docs: https://docs.dev.sync.global/app_dev/token_standard/index.html

FAQ.question: How do I deploy to DevNet/TestNet?
FAQ.answer:
1. dpm build
2. Get validator node (self-host or NaaS)
3. Upload DAR via gRPC/Admin API
4. Set up parties, configure OAuth2/JWT auth
5. Test: LocalNet → DevNet → TestNet → MainNet

Validator: https://docs.dev.sync.global/validator_operator/docker_compose_validator.html

FAQ.question: TypeScript libraries?
FAQ.answer:
1. Generate stubs from JSON API OpenAPI spec (openapi-fetch)
2. dpm codegen-js or dpm codegen-alpha-typescript for Daml bindings
3. @c7/ledger + @c7/react (NOT deprecated @daml/ledger)

Tutorial: https://docs.digitalasset.com/build/3.4/tutorials/json-api/canton_and_the_json_ledger_api_ts.html

FAQ.question: What is Zenith?
FAQ.answer: Shared Reth-based EVM on Canton. EVM payloads wrap into Canton transactions atomically. NOT an L2 rollup. Uses external_call() Daml primitive. Built by Zenith.

FAQ.question: Authentication?
FAQ.answer: Local sandbox: no auth. Production: JWT (RS256). Splice apps: sub=ledger-api-user, aud=configured audience. OAuth2 Authorization Code for UIs. Configure OIDC provider (Auth0, Keycloak).

FAQ.question: Canton Name Service (CNS)?
FAQ.answer: Canton's ENS equivalent. Register names for ~1 USD/90 days. UI: http://cns.validator.HOSTNAME or http://ans.localhost. Implemented in splice-amulet-name-service.

FAQ.question: Featured application?
FAQ.answer:
1. Apply via tokenomics committee form
2. Create FeaturedAppActivityMarker for important events (transfers, mint/burn)
3. ~$1 USD reward per marker (CIP-0078)
4. Self-feature on DevNet for testing
Docs: https://docs.dev.sync.global/app_dev/featured_app_activity_marker.html

FAQ.question: Signatories and observers?
FAQ.answer:
Signatories: required to create/archive. signatory keyword. Every contract needs one. See creation+archival.
Observers: can see, can't act. observer keyword. See create/archive.
Controllers: can exercise a choice. controller keyword. Required authorizers.

FAQ.question: Propose-accept pattern?
FAQ.answer: Most common multi-party pattern. Party A creates Proposal (A=signatory). Party B exercises Accept (adds B's authority). Creates final contract with both signatories. Standard triple: Accept/Reject/Cancel.

FAQ.question: Bridge USDC to Canton?
FAQ.answer:
xReserve UI: https://digital-asset.github.io/xreserve-deposits/
1. Connect wallet (MetaMask)
2. Select MainNet or TestNet
3. Enter USDC amount + Canton party ID
4. Approve 2 ETH transactions
5. Wait ~15 min for ETH finality
Party ID must match onboarding ID.

FAQ.question: What is PQS?
FAQ.answer:
Participant Query Store — SQL read layer for Canton. Ingests ledger data into PostgreSQL.

Install: dpm install <version> (includes scribe.jar)
Locate jar: dpm resolve | grep scribe
Docker: europe-docker.pkg.dev/da-images/public/docker/participant-query-store:<version>

Basic usage:
./scribe.jar pipeline ledger postgres-document \
  --pipeline-filter-parties 'Alice::*' \
  --pipeline-ledger-start Latest \
  --source-ledger-host canton-host --source-ledger-port 6865 \
  --target-postgres-host pg-host --target-postgres-database mydb

Requires: JDK 17+, PostgreSQL 14+. Needs 4GB RAM (PQS) + 8GB RAM (PostgreSQL).
Docs: https://docs.digitalasset.com/build/3.4/tools/pqs

FAQ.question: How do I query the Scan API?
FAQ.answer:
Scan API is on SV nodes. Discovery: https://sync.global/sv-network/

Registry info:
curl https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/registry/metadata/v1/info

Instruments:
curl https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/registry/metadata/v1/instruments

Transfer factory (build transfers):
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/registry/transfer-instruction/v1/transfer-factory -d '{...}'

CC price (OpenMiningRound):
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/api/scan/v0 -d '{"cached_open_mining_round_trip_with_uses":{}}'

For BFT: query 2/3+ of SV scans.

FAQ.question: Splice LocalNet setup?
FAQ.answer:
Option 1 (CN Quickstart): git clone https://github.com/digital-asset/cn-quickstart && make install && make start

Option 2 (Splice bundle):
1. Download from Splice releases
2. tar xzvf 0.5.10_splice-node.tar.gz
3. export LOCALNET_DIR=$PWD/splice-node/docker-compose/localnet IMAGE_TAG=0.5.10
4. docker compose --env-file $LOCALNET_DIR/compose.env ... up -d

UIs: wallet.localhost:2000 (user), wallet.localhost:3000 (provider), scan.localhost:4000
Default users: app-user, app-provider, sv
Swagger: --profile swagger-ui (port 9090)

FAQ.question: Traffic fees?
FAQ.answer:
Canton uses traffic fees, not gas.

Free tier: burstAmount bytes per burstWindow.
Extra: USD/MB in CC at current exchange rate (SV median vote).

Check CC price:
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/api/scan/v0 -d '{"cached_open_mining_round_trip_with_uses":{}}'

Buy traffic: MemberTraffic contracts updated atomically when CC spent.
Docs: https://docs.dev.sync.global/tokenomics/traffic_fees.html

FAQ.question: How to check balance / get holdings?
FAQ.answer:
Holdings are UTXOs. Sum all Holding contracts for owner + instrumentId.

1. Get instrumentId:
curl https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/registry/metadata/v1/instruments

2. Get offset:
curl http://localhost:7575/v2/state/ledger-end

3. Query ACS:
curl http://localhost:7575/v2/state/active-contracts -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d @acs.json

Filter by Holding interface. Sum amounts for balance.

FAQ.question: Multi-package Daml project?
FAQ.answer:
Create multi-package.yaml at project root:

packages:
  - ./core
  - ./tests

Each has own daml.yaml. 'dpm build --all' builds topologically.
'dpm build' in sub-package auto-detects deps.

Best practice: production templates in one package, test scripts separate.

FAQ.question: How do I build a transfer on Canton?
FAQ.answer:
Use Token Standard (CIP-0056) TransferFactory:

1. Get TransferFactory + context from Scan:
curl -X POST https://scan.sv-1.global.canton.network.sync.global/registry/transfer-instruction/v1/transfer-factory -d '{"choiceArguments":{"expectedAdmin":"DSO::...","transfer":{"sender":"...","receiver":"...","amount":"100.0","instrumentId":{"admin":"DSO::...","id":"Amulet"},...},...}}'

2. Returns factoryId, transferKind (direct/2-step), choiceContext, disclosedContracts
3. Submit via JSON Ledger API with disclosed contracts

Docs: https://docs.dev.sync.global/app_dev/token_standard/index.html
